en.extract:
The Micronesian rufous fantail (Rhipidura versicolor) is a species of bird in the family Rhipiduridae that is endemic to the Mariana Islands and the island of Yap in Micronesia.  It was formerly considered to be a subspecies of the Australian rufous fantail (Rhipidura rufifrons).


Taxonomy
The Micronesian rufous fantail was formally described in 1872 by the German ornithologists Gustav Hartlaub and Otto Finsch based on specimens collected by the Polish naturalist John Stanislaw Kubary on the island of Yap in Micronesia. They placed it with the fantails in the genus Rhipidura  and coined the binomial name Rhipidura versicolor. The specific epithet is Latin meaning "various colours" (from vertere, "to change" and color "colour"). The Micronesian rufous fantail was formerly considered to be a subspecies of the rufous fantail (renamed the Australian rufous fantail) (Rhipidura rufifrons) but is now treated as a separate species mainly based on the genetic differences.
Four subspecies are recognised:

R. v. uraniae Oustalet, 1881 – Guam (south Mariana Islands, west Micronesia) this subspecies is Extinct
R. v. saipanensis Hartert, EJO, 1898 – Saipan, Tinian and Aguiguan (south Northern Mariana Islands, west Micronesia)
R. v. mariae Baker, RH, 1946 – Rota (south Northern Mariana Islands, west Micronesia)
R. v. versicolor Hartlaub & Finsch, 1872 – Yap (west Caroline Islands)


References

The '''Micronesian rufous fantail''' ('''''Rhipidura versicolor''''') is a [[species]] of bird in the family [[Rhipiduridae]] that is endemic to the [[Mariana Islands]] and the island of [[Yap]] in [[Micronesia]].  It was formerly considered to be a [[subspecies]] of the [[Australian rufous fantail]] (''Rhipidura rufifrons'').

==Taxonomy==
The Micronesian rufous fantail was [[Species description|formally described]] in 1872 by the German ornithologists [[Gustav Hartlaub]] and [[Otto Finsch]] based on specimens collected by the Polish naturalist [[John Stanislaw Kubary]] on the island of [[Yap]] in [[Micronesia]]. They placed it with the fantails in the [[genus]] ''[[Rhipidura ]]'' and coined the [[binomial name]] ''Rhipidura versicolor''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Hartlaub | first1=Gustav |author1-link=Gustav Hartlaub | last2=Finsch | first2=Otto | author2-link=Otto Finsch | date=1872 | title=On a fourth collection of birds from the Pelew and Mackenzie Islands | journal=Proceedings of the Zoological Society of London | volume=1872 | pages=87–114 [96–97] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/28611446 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Cottrell | editor2-first=G. William | year=1986 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=11 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=556 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14484257 }}</ref> The specific epithet is [[Latin]] meaning "various colours" (from ''vertere'', "to change" and ''color'' "colour").<ref>{{cite book | last=Jobling | first=James A. | year=2010| title=The Helm Dictionary of Scientific Bird Names | publisher=Christopher Helm | location=London | isbn=978-1-4081-2501-4 | page=400 | url=https://archive.org/stream/Helm_Dictionary_of_Scientific_Bird_Names_by_James_A._Jobling#page/n400/mode/1up }}</ref> The Micronesian rufous fantail was formerly considered to be a [[subspecies]] of the rufous fantail (renamed the [[Australian rufous fantail]]) (''Rhipidura rufifrons'') but is now treated as a separate species mainly based on the genetic differences.<ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Klicka | first1=L.B. | last2=Campillo | first2=L.C. | last3=Manthey | first3=J.D. | last4=Andersen | first4=M.J. | last5=Dumbacher | first5=J.P. | last6=Filardi | first6=C.E. | last7=Joseph | first7=L. | last8=Uy | first8=J.A.C. | last9=Weidemann | first9=D.E. | last10=Moyle | first10=R.G. | date=2023 | title=Genomic and geographic diversification of a 'great-speciator' (''Rhipidura rufifrons'') | journal=Ornithology | volume=140 | issue=1 | article-number=ukac049 | doi=10.1093/ornithology/ukac049 | doi-access=free }}</ref><ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=December 2023 | title=Orioles, drongos, fantails | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/orioles/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=20 January 2024}}</ref>

Four subspecies are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''R. v. uraniae'' [[Émile Oustalet|Oustalet]], 1881 – [[Guam]] (south [[Mariana Islands]], west [[Micronesia]]) this subspecies is [[Extinct]]
* ''R. v. saipanensis'' [[Ernst Hartert|Hartert, EJO]], 1898 – [[Saipan]], [[Tinian]] and [[Aguiguan]] (south [[Northern Mariana Islands]], west Micronesia)
* ''R. v. mariae'' Baker, RH, 1946 – [[Rota (island)|Rota]] (south Northern Mariana Islands, west Micronesia)
* ''R. v. versicolor'' [[Gustav Hartlaub|Hartlaub]] & [[Otto Finsch|Finsch]], 1872 – [[Yap]] (west [[Caroline Islands]])
